text: Agreed, but I see math as but a tool for simulating 4D phenomena on a 2D medium (papyrus). It is no different, IMO, from using computer simulations. If one expresses a wrong understanding in a way that is consistent in math, the math might still be “correct”, but the understanding is still “wrong”. For example, humanity spent 1,000s of years wasting time and effort believing that the sun orbited the earth while their maths and notations did not alert them to the fundamental mistake.
